2604.24625.title: Meta-CoT: Enhancing Granularity and Generalization in Image Editing
2604.24625.paper_summary: Unified multi-modal understanding/generative models have shown improved image editing performance by incorporating fine-grained understanding into their Chain-of-Thought (CoT) process. However, a critical question remains underexplored: what forms of CoT and training strategy can jointly enhance both the understanding granularity and generalization? To address this, we propose Meta-CoT, a paradigm that performs a two-level decomposition of any single-image editing operation with two key properties: (1) Decomposability. We observe that any editing intention can be represented as a triplet - (task, target, required understanding ability). Inspired by this, Meta-CoT decomposes both the editing task and the target, generating task-specific CoT and traversing editing operations on all targets. This decomposition enhances the model's understanding granularity of editing operations and guides it to learn each element of the triplet during training, substantially improving the editing capability. (2) Generalizability. In the second decomposition level, we further break down editing tasks into five fundamental meta-tasks. We find that training on these five meta-tasks, together with the other two elements of the triplet, is sufficient to achieve strong generalization across diverse, unseen editing tasks. To further align the model's editing behavior with its CoT reasoning, we introduce the CoT-Editing Consistency Reward, which encourages more accurate and effective utilization of CoT information during editing. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves an overall 15.8% improvement across 21 editing tasks, and generalizes effectively to unseen editing tasks when trained on only a small set of meta-tasks. Our code, benchmark, and model are released at https://shiyi-zh0408.github.io/projectpages/Meta-CoT/
2604.24625.paper_summary_zh: 統一的多模態理解/生成模型通過將細緻的理解納入其思維鏈（CoT）過程中，顯示出改進的圖像編輯性能。<lb/>然而，一個關鍵問題仍然未被充分探索：什麼形式的CoT和訓練策略可以共同增強理解的細緻度和泛化能力？<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了Meta-CoT，一種對任何單圖像編輯操作進行兩級分解的範式，具有兩個關鍵特性：<lb/>(1) 可分解性。我們觀察到，任何編輯意圖都可以表示為一個三元組 - （任務、目標、所需理解能力）。<lb/>受到這一點的啟發，Meta-CoT對編輯任務和目標進行分解，生成任務特定的CoT並遍歷所有目標上的編輯操作。<lb/>這種分解增強了模型對編輯操作的理解細緻度，並指導其在訓練過程中學習三元組的每個元素，顯著提高了編輯能力。<lb/>(2) 泛化能力。在第二級分解中，我們進一步將編輯任務分解為五個基本的元任務。<lb/>我們發現，對這五個元任務進行訓練，加上三元組的其他兩個元素，足以在多樣的、未見過的編輯任務中實現強大的泛化能力。<lb/>為了進一步使模型的編輯行為與其CoT推理對齊，我們引入了CoT-編輯一致性獎勵，這鼓勵在編輯過程中更準確和有效地利用CoT信息。<lb/>實驗表明，我們的方法在21個編輯任務中實現了整體15.8%的改進，並在僅訓練於一小組元任務時，能有效地泛化到未見過的編輯任務。<lb/>我們的代碼、基準和模型已發布於 https://shiyi-zh0408.github.io/projectpages/Meta-CoT/
2604.24625.author: Shiyi Zhang et.al.
2604.24625.authors: Shiyi Zhang, Yiji Cheng, Tiankai Hang, Zijin Yin, Runze He, Yu Xu, Wenxun Dai, Yunlong Lin, Chunyu Wang, Qinglin Lu, Yansong Tang
